description: HPFP failure — low fuel pressure, lean codes
expected_behavior: Agent should identify elevated STFT and LTFT on both banks (>10%) as a lean condition, note the low relative fuel pressure (010A ~181 kPa vs healthy ~340+ kPa), and call out HPFP failure as the likely root cause given the BMW N54 known quirk. Urgency should be HIGH or CRITICAL.
failure_mode: Weak prompt diagnoses generic 'lean condition' without connecting low fuel pressure to HPFP failure or referencing the N54 quirk.
alldata_note: AllData would show TSB #13-11-4-214-978 for N54 HPFP replacement procedure.

description: Charge pipe blow-off — sudden lean spike then recovery
expected_behavior: Agent should notice high IAT std (charge air heating after blow-off event), STFT max spikes to 24%, wide STFT variance on both banks indicating intermittent lean events under boost. Should reference charge pipe blow-out as a known N54 failure mode. Urgency HIGH.
failure_mode: Weak prompt flags lean codes without noting the intermittent nature (high std) or connecting IAT spike to a charge pipe event.
alldata_note: AllData charge pipe inspection procedure for N54.

description: GDI carbon buildup — mild idle lean, clears under load
expected_behavior: Agent should identify mild lean trims (~9-11% LTFT) concentrated at idle/low-load, normal post-cat O2. Should reference GDI carbon buildup on intake valves as a known N54 issue (no port wash, walnut blasting needed). Urgency MEDIUM.
failure_mode: Weak prompt suggests vacuum leak without considering GDI carbon buildup pattern that affects low-RPM mixture.
alldata_note: AllData walnut blasting procedure for N54 intake valves.

description: Cold-start wastegate rattle — not a fault condition
expected_behavior: Agent should recognize cold-start context (ECT 38°C, vehicle stationary, idle RPM), all sensor values within normal ranges, fuel trims healthy. Should assign LOW or NORMAL urgency and note that N54 wastegate rattle at cold start is a known benign quirk.
failure_mode: Weak prompt over-triages cold-start normal variance as a problem, assigns MEDIUM urgency.
alldata_note: AllData N54 wastegate rattle TSB — informational only, no repair required.

description: Healthy BMW at highway cruise — all values nominal
expected_behavior: All sensors within healthy ranges. Fuel trims ±5%, O2 sensors oscillating correctly, ECT stable at 91°C, MAF and MAP appropriate for highway cruise. Agent should assign NORMAL urgency with no action items.
failure_mode: Weak prompt finds spurious issues in normal variance, assigns MEDIUM urgency unnecessarily.
alldata_note: No service needed.

description: ECT sensor stuck at 255°C — bogus out-of-range reading
expected_behavior: Agent should flag ECT 255°C as an impossible/bogus reading (max human-survivable engine coolant is ~130°C; 255 is the 0xFF rollover sentinel for many adapters). Should note the sensor is unreliable and request a re-scan or ECT sensor replacement. Other sensors are healthy — urgency should be MEDIUM for the bad sensor, not CRITICAL for the vehicle.
failure_mode: Weak prompt takes 255°C ECT at face value and diagnoses severe engine overheating, recommending immediate shutdown — a dangerous over-reaction to a stuck sensor.
alldata_note: 0xFF ECT rollover is a common ELM327 adapter artifact.

description: MAF sensor missing — only MAP pressure available for load estimation
expected_behavior: Agent should note that MAF (PID 0110) is absent from this snapshot even though this vehicle uses MAF-based fueling. Should flag incomplete data, recommend confirming MAF sensor connectivity or re-scanning with a BMW-compatible tool. Cannot fully assess fueling accuracy without MAF data.
failure_mode: Weak prompt analyzes available sensors without noting the critical MAF gap for a MAF-metered engine.
alldata_note: AllData wiring diagram for N54 MAF sensor circuit.

description: Bank 1 O2 sensor flat — possible O2 sensor failure or heater circuit fault
expected_behavior: B1S1 O2 sensor (0114) shows nearly flat voltage (std 0.005V) — a healthy narrowband sensor oscillates with std ~0.25-0.30V. B1 trims are running rich (STFT -6%, LTFT -8%) because ECU is commanding rich to compensate for a perceived signal. B2 is normal. Agent should diagnose B1S1 O2 sensor failure and assign HIGH urgency.
failure_mode: Weak prompt flags rich condition on B1 without diagnosing the stuck O2 sensor as the root cause.
alldata_note: AllData O2 sensor replacement procedure for N54 Bank 1 Sensor 1.

description: Overboost condition — MAP pressure beyond normal operating range
expected_behavior: MAP hitting 228-248 kPa absolute is overboost territory for the N54 (stock target ~180 kPa at full throttle). IAT elevated from charge heat. Fuel trims normal (ECU correcting correctly). Agent should flag overboost as HIGH urgency, recommend checking wastegate solenoid and boost controller.
failure_mode: Weak prompt focuses on IAT elevation from boost heat rather than identifying the overboost root cause.
alldata_note: AllData wastegate actuator replacement and WGFV solenoid testing procedure.

description: Bank 1 lean only — possible vacuum leak upstream of B1 throttle body
expected_behavior: B1 STFT/LTFT strongly lean (~14%), B2 completely normal. Single-bank lean asymmetry should lead to vacuum leak diagnosis affecting B1 intake side, or a B1-specific injector issue. Agent should not diagnose a fuel system-wide problem. Urgency HIGH.
failure_mode: Weak prompt diagnoses system-wide lean without noting the bank asymmetry, missing the localized root cause.
alldata_note: AllData intake manifold vacuum diagram for N54, bank 1 specific.

description: EGR valve stuck open — rough idle, lean fuel trims
expected_behavior: EGR commanded at 62% at idle (should be near 0% at idle), EGR error -18% (actual flow exceeds commanded). STFT/LTFT lean because exhaust gas dilutes charge. Agent should diagnose stuck-open EGR valve, reference known Honda EGR quirk. Urgency HIGH.
failure_mode: Weak prompt diagnoses lean condition from fuel system without noting the EGR commanded/error mismatch.
alldata_note: AllData P0400 EGR flow diagnostic and EGR valve replacement procedure for L15B.

description: EGR valve stuck closed — elevated NOx, emissions failure
expected_behavior: EGR commanded shows near zero output even during cruise conditions where EGR should be active (typically 10-30% commanded at light load). Fuel trims healthy so combustion is fine, but EGR not functioning means elevated combustion temps and NOx. Agent should diagnose stuck-closed EGR with emissions implications. Urgency MEDIUM (vehicle runs OK, emissions issue).
failure_mode: Weak prompt sees normal fuel trims and assigns NORMAL urgency, missing the inactive EGR system.
alldata_note: AllData EGR flow test procedure for Honda L15B.

description: Healthy Honda Fit at city driving — all values nominal
expected_behavior: All systems normal. Fuel trims within ±5%, O2 sensor oscillating correctly, EGR commanded at reasonable light-load values with small error. Agent should assign NORMAL urgency.
failure_mode: Weak prompt over-reads EGR error variance as a fault.
alldata_note: No service needed.

description: CVT temperature not available in generic OBD2 — missing PID for heat sensitivity
expected_behavior: IAT at 38°C (warm day, stop-and-go traffic). Engine sensors normal. However, ATF/CVT temperature is not available via generic OBD2. Given known Honda CVT heat sensitivity above 230°F (110°C) and warm ambient conditions, agent should recommend enabling Honda-proprietary CVT temp PID if possible, or noting the data gap. Urgency LOW but data completeness concern noted.
failure_mode: Weak prompt analyzes visible sensors only without noting the missing CVT temp data gap for a vehicle with known CVT heat sensitivity.
alldata_note: Honda proprietary CVT ATF temp PID requires enhanced scan tool.

description: Rich condition — negative STFT/LTFT, possible injector drip or high fuel pressure
expected_behavior: B1 STFT -13%, LTFT -12% — persistent rich condition. O2 B1S1 biased high (mean 0.62V, should be ~0.45V oscillating). EGR inactive at idle. Agent should diagnose injector drip or fuel pressure regulator failure as probable causes. Urgency HIGH.
failure_mode: Weak prompt mentions 'rich mixture' without identifying the fuel delivery root cause or noting the O2 sensor bias.
alldata_note: AllData injector leak-down test and fuel pressure regulator test for L15B.

description: MAP sensor reading 255 kPa — adapter rollover artifact
expected_behavior: MAP at constant 255 kPa with std=0 is a stuck/bogus reading (Honda Fit naturally-aspirated at sea level should be 30-100 kPa). Also note the Honda proprietary scanner misread warning. Agent should flag the MAP reading as unreliable, note the 0xFF rollover artifact pattern, and recommend using a Honda-compatible scan tool. Urgency MEDIUM for bad sensor data.
failure_mode: Weak prompt treats 255 kPa MAP as a boost/overboost condition on a naturally-aspirated vehicle.
alldata_note: Honda-proprietary scan tools (HDS) recommended for accurate MAP readings on this vehicle.

description: Sluggish O2 sensor — slow switching, fuel trim compensation
expected_behavior: B1S1 O2 std=0.14V is lower than healthy (should be ~0.27V), indicating slow switching. STFT showing higher variance than normal as ECU hunts trying to correct. Post-cat O2 normal. Agent should diagnose sluggish upstream O2 sensor heading toward failure. Urgency MEDIUM.
failure_mode: Weak prompt sees marginal trims and assigns LOW urgency without examining O2 switching speed via std.
alldata_note: AllData O2 sensor response time test procedure.

description: High idle load — possible IAC or throttle body carbon buildup
expected_behavior: Engine load at idle is 42% (typical healthy idle for L15B is ~18-25%). MAP at 68 kPa at idle is high (should be 30-40 kPa at idle indicating vacuum). RPM stable. Trims slightly rich. Pattern consistent with IAC stuck open or throttle body carbon buildup admitting excess air that ECU compensates with enrichment. Urgency MEDIUM.
failure_mode: Weak prompt attributes high load to an AC compressor or other accessory load without examining MAP/load correlation.
alldata_note: AllData throttle body cleaning and IAC test procedure for L15B.

description: EGR PIDs missing from snapshot — cannot assess EGR health
expected_behavior: All available sensors look healthy. However, EGR commanded (012C) and EGR error (012D) are absent even though this vehicle has an EGR system. Agent should flag the missing EGR PIDs, note this vehicle is expected to have these PIDs, and request re-scan with EGR monitoring enabled. Visible health is NORMAL but data gap is significant.
failure_mode: Weak prompt assigns NORMAL urgency without noting the missing EGR data for a vehicle that uses EGR.
alldata_note: Honda Fit 2015 has EGR system; PIDs 012C and 012D should be present.

description: Secondary air injection pump failure — morning cold-start P0418 pattern
expected_behavior: Cold start (ECT 42°C), lean trims on both banks (~6-8% LTFT). Secondary air injection pump should be active during cold start but pattern suggests it's not pumping (lean without the normal SAI-induced lean spike pattern expected). Agent should reference the Tundra's known SAI pump failure after 100k miles. Urgency MEDIUM.
failure_mode: Weak prompt diagnoses generic lean cold-start without connecting to SAI system failure known on this vehicle.
alldata_note: AllData P0418 SAI pump relay test and replacement procedure for 2UR-FSE.

description: Bank 2 O2 sensor heat damage — erratic B2S1 voltage
expected_behavior: B2S1 O2 shows abnormally high std (0.38V vs normal ~0.27V) and wider voltage swings (0.02-0.98V) — erratic switching pattern. B2 trims running rich (-7 to -8% LTFT) as ECU chases the erratic signal. B1 completely normal. Agent should diagnose B2S1 O2 sensor damage and reference the known Tundra wiring heat damage near B2 exhaust manifold. Urgency HIGH.
failure_mode: Weak prompt diagnoses B2 rich condition without identifying the erratic O2 sensor as the root cause or the known heat damage issue.
alldata_note: AllData Tundra B2S1 O2 sensor wiring heat shield inspection and sensor replacement.

description: EVAP small leak likely from aftermarket gas cap
expected_behavior: All sensors nominal. Context: P0442 small EVAP leak DTC was present (noted in scenario). Agent should assign LOW urgency, note the Tundra's known EVAP code trigger from aftermarket gas caps, and recommend verifying OEM cap before further diagnostics. Not an engine performance concern.
failure_mode: Weak prompt over-triages EVAP leak as HIGH urgency without noting the benign aftermarket gas cap quirk.
alldata_note: AllData P0442 EVAP small leak diagnostic — check gas cap seal first.

description: Healthy Tundra at highway cruise — all values nominal
expected_behavior: All sensors in healthy range. Both banks show normal fuel trims, O2 sensors oscillating correctly, ECT stable at 90°C, MAF appropriate for highway cruise. Agent should assign NORMAL urgency with no action items.
failure_mode: Weak prompt finds spurious issues in normal variance.
alldata_note: No service needed.

description: Both banks lean — possible MAF sensor contamination
expected_behavior: Both banks lean symmetrically (~11-13% LTFT). MAF reading appears low relative to load (low g/s for given engine load and RPM). Symmetric bank lean on a MAF-metered V8 points to MAF sensor contamination or air filter restriction rather than injector or O2 issues. Agent should diagnose MAF sensor as primary suspect. Urgency HIGH.
failure_mode: Weak prompt diagnoses 'lean condition' without differentiating symmetric MAF fault from a fuel-side issue.
alldata_note: AllData MAF sensor cleaning procedure and replacement for 2UR-FSE.

description: Bank 1 rich, Bank 2 lean — injector imbalance across cylinder banks
expected_behavior: B1 rich (-9 to -11% LTFT) while B2 lean (+10 to +12% LTFT). B1S1 O2 biased high (mean 0.58V). This is a classic injector imbalance pattern — one bank over-fueling, other bank under-fueling. Asymmetric trims suggest injector(s) on B1 are leaking or high-flowing, or a fuel distribution imbalance. Urgency HIGH.
failure_mode: Weak prompt treats rich and lean separately rather than identifying the cross-bank imbalance pattern.
alldata_note: AllData injector balance test procedure for 2UR-FSE.
